title: -Contracts: Matrix HG Inc. and Prime Mechanical Service for Heating, Ventilation, and Air Conditioning (HVAC) Maintenance and Repair

sections.recommendation:
Adopt two Resolutions authorizing the City Manager to:
1. Execute a contract with any amendments with Matrix HG Inc. to provide HVAC
repairs and maintenance services for a three-year period from September 1,
2025 to August 31, 2028, in an amount not to exceed $150,000, with an option to
extend for two additional one-year terms.
2. Execute a contract with any amendments with Prime Mechanical Service to
provide HVAC repairs and maintenance services for a three-year period from
September 1, 2025 to August 31, 2028, in an amount not to exceed $150,000,
with an option to extend for two additional one-year terms.
sections.fiscal_impacts:
Funding necessary to support these contracts is available in the FY2026 Public Works
Building Purchases and Management Fund (Fund 636) and Building Maintenance Fund
(Fund 673) Funding for future years is subject to Council’s approval of the proposed
citywide budget for Fiscal Years 2027-2028.
sections.current_situation:
The existing contract for HVAC maintenance and repair was executed in 2015. To
ensure compliance with City procurement policies and to avoid disruption in HVAC
maintenance and repair services, it is necessary to execute a new contract based on
the results of the recent competitive solicitation. HVAC maintenance and repair are
critical to supporting the air quality and temperature control needs of City staff and
community members.
The City conducted Invitation to Bid Specification No. 25-11724-C with Proposals due
no later than May 15, 2025. The City received four proposals in response. The selection
committee, composed of subject matter experts from Public Works, evaluated the

Matrix HG Inc. and Prime Mechanical Service for Heating, Ventilation, CONSENT CALENDAR
and Air Conditioning (HVAC) Maintenance and Repair July 29, 2025
proposals and reported that three were responsive bids with competitive rates within the
City’s budget.
The evaluation criteria used to rate the proposals were as follows: Project Approach
(30%), Expertise and References (15%), Cost (25%), and Ability to Perform Services
Scope (30%). Matrix HG Inc. and Prime Mechanical Service were selected as the
highest-scoring, responsive vendors.
These services support the City’s facilities in alignment with the Strategic Plan Priority of
advancing our goal to provide state-of-the-art, well-maintained infrastructure, amenities,
and facilities.
sections.background:
The City uses HVAC systems to provide a healthy and comfortable environment, while
also regulating extreme heat or cold weather conditions. HVAC systems require routine
maintenance to ensure optimal performance.
The selected vendors have the adequate staff and equipment necessary to provide City
services. The new contracts will ensure clear guidelines and standardized prices for
HVAC maintenance and repair services.
ENVIRONMENTAL SUSTAINABILITY AND CLIMATE IMPACTS
Regular maintenance and repair of HVAC systems is essential for optimizing
performance, prolonging equipment lifespan, and minimizing the environmental impacts.
Well-maintained HVAC systems operate more efficiently, which leads to reduced energy
consumption and carbon emissions. Regular servicing prevents the leaking of
refrigerants into the atmosphere, which are damaging to the ozone layer and contribute
to climate change. Finally, optimized HVAC systems also improve indoor air quality and
provide air circulation benefits.
sections.rationale:
The City requires reliable and responsive HVAC maintenance and repair services to
maintain the heating, ventilation and air-conditioning needs of City facilities.
Executing new contracts ensures ongoing service and prevents potential disruption
to fleet-dependent City operations.
The new HVAC maintenance and repair services contracts will address the City’s
needs, while stabilizing costs and fees. The selected HVAC service providers will be
utilized as needed to ensure the City has adequate resources available for HVAC
maintenance and repair.
sections.alternatives:
The City does not have existing staffing with the training or expertise to perform HVAC
maintenance and repair work. Therefore, there are no viable alternatives at this time to
contract services.

RESOLUTION NO. ##,###-N.S.
CONTRACT: MATRIX HG INC. FOR HEATING, VENTILATION, AND AIR
CONDITIONING (HVAC) MAINTENANCE AND REPAIR
WHEREAS, the City conducted RFP Solicitation No. 25-11724-C with bids due no later
than May 15, 2025; and
WHEREAS, the City received four proposals and a selection committee, composed of
subject matter experts from the Public Works Department, met to evaluate the bids; and
WHEREAS, Matrix HG Inc. and Prime Mechanical Service were selected as the most
responsive and highest-scoring vendors; and
WHEREAS, funding is available in the Fiscal Year 2026 adopted budget in the Public
Works Facilities Division Building Purchases and Management-Fund 636 and Building
Maintenance-Fund 673. Funding for future years is subject to Council’s approval of the
proposed citywide budget for Fiscal Years 2027-2028.
NOW THEREFORE, BE IT RESOLVED by the Council of the City of Berkeley that the
City Manager is authorized to execute a contract and any amendments with Matrix HG Inc.
to provide HVAC maintenance and repair services for three years from September 1,
2025 to August 31, 2028 for an amount not to exceed $150,000 with the option to extend
for two additional one-year terms.
